channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: En Roswell, los recientes archivos ovni desclasificados decepcionan
item.description: Se suponía que el festival de ovnis de Roswell, celebrado durante el fin de semana del 4 de julio, iba a ser una dulce reivindicación para miles de ufólogos. En cambio, se lamentaron del encubrimiento que temían que nunca acabará.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault and Paul Ratje

media:content.media:description: Había personas esperando que empezara un desfile durante el festival anual de ovnis en Roswell, Nuevo México.

item.title: Joe Hisaishi, la voz musical de Studio Ghibli, retoma su amor por la música clásica
item.description: Hisaishi, cuya obra ayudó a definir la sensibilidad de las películas de Miyazaki, está redirigiendo su carrera hacia conciertos y grabaciones con grandes orquestas.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Barone

media:content.media:credit: Kentaro Takahashi para The New York Times

item.title: Cómo los medicamentos GLP-1 alimentan nuestra cultura enferma
item.description: En un entorno que premia la delgadez, recurrir a estos fármacos puede parecer una elección perfectamente lógica.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Grose

item.title: Typhoon Bavi Hits China, Causing Evacuations, Floods and Flight Delays
item.description: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China after killing 17 in the Philippines, skirting Japan and hitting Taiwan. At its largest, the storm was about the width of France.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and Pei-Lin Wu

media:content.media:credit: Go Nakamura/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Fallen trees seen on Sunday in Wenzhou, Zhejiang Province, where Typhoon Bavi made its second landfall around midnight.

item.title: La fiebre por las bananas
item.description: La fruta fresca más consumida de Estados Unidos ha irrumpido con una fuerza inesperada en los menús de las cafeterías. ¿Será el próximo “sabor de temporada”?
item.dc:creator: Luke Fortney

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland para The New York Times. Estilista de atrezo: Megumi Emoto.
media:content.media:description: La popularidad del plátano lo ha convertido en un ingrediente ideal para experimentar con bebidas y bocadillos.

item.title: Younger Voters Are Propelling the Democratic Socialist Surge in New York
item.description: Age seemed to be the predominant factor in the Democratic Socialists of America’s primary wins in House races in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg and Luke Vrotsos

media:content.media:credit: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani helped elevate the candidacies of three leftist candidates who won House primaries last month. From left to right, Claire Valdez, Brad Lander, Mr. Mamdani and Darializa Avila Chevalier.

item.title: The Democratic Socialists Winning Elections Far From New York City
item.description: They won Democratic state legislative primaries in Buffalo and Syracuse, showing how the party’s messaging and ground game can work outside New York City.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Mark Sommer

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Petracca for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Adam Bojak, a democratic socialist candidate, won a three-way Democratic primary for the New York State Assembly in the Buffalo area.

item.title: Tracee Ellis Ross Is Back in New York City, Living a Dream Come True
item.description: The actress and entrepreneur got an apartment in the city for the summer while she makes her Broadway debut in “Every Brilliant Thing.”
item.dc:creator: Addie Morfoot

media:content.media:credit: Sabrina Santiago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tracee Ellis Ross is in New York City for three months as she makes her Broadway debut. She stopped by Health Harmony in Greenwich Village for some groceries.

item.title: ‘I Lifted One Arm, Palmed the Ball and Shot It Straight Back’
item.description: More Metropolitan Diary friends, including the astrophysicist Neil deGrasse Tyson and the actress Rebecca Wisocky, share their New York stories to help mark the column’s 50th anniversary.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answer July 12, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Senator Lindsey Graham, Republican of South Carolina, Dies at 71
item.description: Mr. Graham died of a “brief and sudden” illness on Saturday evening, his office said. He served in the Senate for more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham speaking in Spartanburg, S.C., on June 30.

item.title: Las bacterias del cuerpo tienen un lenguaje. Apenas empezamos a entender lo que dicen
item.description: Aunque el microbioma humano desempeña un papel fundamental en nuestra salud, sigue siendo un territorio poco conocido. Dos investigadores buscan revelar sus secretos.
item.dc:creator: Jeneen Interlandi

item.title: The World Is Cutting Ties With America. It’s Already Costing Us.
item.description: When other countries cut ties, Americans pay.
item.dc:creator: Jon Finer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Naila Ruechel for The New York Times

item.title: Serena Williams Is Back. Thank Goodness.
item.description: Until Serena Williams announced her return to tennis at 44, I never realized how much I needed her.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Goodman

item.title: As India Erases Its Colonial Past, Delhi’s Elite Feel Targeted
item.description: India’s government wants to shut down Delhi’s Gymkhana Club, which the prime minister calls a vestige of colonialism. Some members suspect a different motive.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy W. Peters, Pragati K.B. and Saumya Khandelwal

media:content.media:description: The Delhi Gymkhana Club is one of the oldest in India.

item.title: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
item.description: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bradley, Michael Schwirtz and Adam Goldman

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Aeroflot office in Toranomon Kotohira Tower in Tokyo. Japan’s weak espionage laws and flourishing high-tech industry have made it a crucial base for the Russian war effort.

item.title: No Corrections: July 12, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Sunday, July 12, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Couples Comedy Needed Therapy
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 12, 2026.

item.title: Oil Prices Set for More Volatility After Latest Iranian Attack in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped above prewar levels this week, demonstrating Iran’s capacity to move energy prices by projecting power in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz in June.

item.title: Toronto Police Search For Active Shooter After 2 Killed at Street Festival
item.description: The shooting disrupted a weekend Latino celebration where hundreds had gathered on Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:credit: Cole Burston/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Police officers stand guard as emergency workers respond at the site of a deadly shooting at a salsa-themed street festival in Toronto on Saturday.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Launches Strikes After Iran Fires on Ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said it was responding to the attack with strikes on U.S. assets in the Middle East. The defense ministries of the United Arab Emirates and Qatar both said they were intercepting strikes.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Reuters

item.title: Slight Adjustments
item.description: Collin Drown’s debut Times puzzle doesn’t mince words.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Timm Schamberger/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Warning, solvers: mudslinging ahead.

item.title: White House Directed Patel to Oversee Investigation Involving Times Reporting
item.description: The F.B.I. director spent about eight hours at the White House Friday focused on the effort, which led to the subpoenaing of several Times reporters who wrote about the security of Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Glenn Thrush and Maggie Haberman

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kash Patel’s role in the leak investigation reflects a further dismantling of the wall that had separated the White House and the F.B.I.

item.title: At Vigil, Sons of Man Killed by ICE in Houston Call for Accountability
item.description: The sons of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo recalled their father’s love of soccer and said they would “continue to keep fighting for him.”
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The sons of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo spoke at a vigil held for their father on Saturday morning.

item.title: Maine Democrats Will Replace Graham Platner. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: Mr. Platner’s withdrawal from a marquee Senate race, under pressure from his party, has set in motion an unusual competition to become the new nominee.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson and Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The campaign headquarters of Graham Platner in Ellsworth, Maine, this week.

item.title: Black Hawk Helicopters Rescue Hundreds From Summer Camp During Floods
item.description: The National Guard evacuated campers after flooding in Missouri that reminded some families of the devastating floods at Camp Mystic in central Texas last year.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: KTVI, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Floodwaters from a cluster of thunderstorms that dropped a foot of water in some parts of Missouri.

item.title: La tristeza, la rabia y la loca alegría de la fase eliminatoria del Mundial
item.description: La mejor forma de entender esta fase del torneo es imaginar un túnel de autolavado hecho con ralladores de queso.
item.dc:creator: Sam Anderson and Sara Messinger

item.title: Jayson Conner, 48, and Jeffrey Newman, 58, Die; Gave Thousands of Backpacks to Those in Need
item.description: The couple, who died within a few days of each other, provided needed supplies, like socks and wet wipes, to people living on New York City’s streets.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Inglaterra y Noruega reavivan su rivalidad de cara al Mundial
item.description: Además de los lazos históricos y diplomáticos, los equipos de fútbol de Inglaterra tienen seguidores fieles en Noruega. Este sábado, los aficionados tendrán que elegir un bando.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los aficionados noruegos celebraban tras el partido en East Rutherford, Nueva Jersey, el domingo.

item.title: Trump’s Posts on Singing Somali Schoolchildren Stir Anger in Minnesota
item.description: The state’s large Muslim and Somali communities expressed indignation after the president reposted a video of a kindergarten promotion ceremony, including comments noting the girls were in hijabs.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Dylan Freedman

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday, the day he posted about the children in Minnesota.

item.title: A California Man Took a Selfie at a Crime Scene. It Led to His Arrest.
item.description: Two men were taken into custody this week after $100,000 in tools, copper and vehicles was taken from a business in the Napa Valley. A selfie that one of them took served as evidence.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner

item.title: ¿Matt Damon parece griego? No. Entonces deja de quejarte de Lupita Nyong’o
item.description: Helena de Troya era una belleza legendaria, pero también mucho más que eso.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Mendelsohn

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración de Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: Videos Shed Light on Pursuit Before ICE Killing in Houston
item.description: Surveillance footage obtained by The Times shows ICE agents driving aggressively in unmarked vehicles, but the moment of the fatal shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo remains murky.
item.dc:creator: Robin Stein, Devon Lum, Sam Lerma, Mimi Dwyer, Alexander Cardia, Aric Toler, Dmitriy Khavin, Charlie Smart and Allison McCann

item.title: Trump-Appointed Judge Reluctantly Grants Dismissal of Proud Boys Case
item.description: Judge Timothy J. Kelly, a federal judge in Washington, noted that he had little choice but to accept the Trump administration’s move to end the case.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Supporters of President Trump storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Have You Made This Cake Yet?
item.description: This simple strawberry spoon cake is currently our most popular strawberry dessert on New York Times Cooking, and it’s easy to see why.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Andrew Purcell for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Carrie Purcell.
media:content.media:description: Jerrelle Guy’s strawberry spoon cake.

item.title: A Wildfire Killed 12 People in Southern Spain. Here’s the Latest.
item.description: Officials said more than 20 people were still missing because of the fire, one of the deadliest in Spain’s history. Most of those confirmed to have been killed were from Belgium or Britain.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Barragán and Lynsey Chutel

media:content.media:credit: Pablo Blazquez Dominguez/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A wildfire moving toward residential buildings in El Pocico, Spain, on Friday.

item.title: We Have 7-Eleven Snacks at Home
item.description: Skip the prepackaged stuff and make your own Pop-Tarts and Hostess cupcakes at home.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Armando Rafael for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Simon Andrews.
media:content.media:description: Margaux Laskey’s homemade Pop-Tarts.

item.title: ¿Pasas mucho tiempo en el teléfono? Estas sugerencias pueden servirte
item.description: Nuestros lectores han compartido algunas estrategias que les han servido para reducir su tiempo con las pantallas.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: 15 Killed as Speedboat Capsizes in Vietnam
item.description: A speedboat was carrying 36 people when it overturned near the island of Phu Quoc in southern Vietnam. The other people onboard were rescued alive.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai and Tung Ngo

media:content.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Rescue operations after a tourist vessel capsized off Phu Quoc island in southern Vietnam on Saturday.

item.title: Inside the Secret Factory That Supplies Ukraine’s War Drones
item.description: With its mass-produced and inexpensive A.I. powered war machines, the German start-up Helsing SE illustrates a profound shift in military spending.
item.dc:creator: Vivienne Walt

media:content.media:credit: Patrick Junker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Helsing SE, which makes autonomous jets and drones, is the most valuable A.I. defense start-up in Europe.

item.title: In Sudan, a Preventable Atrocity
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of people in Sudan are bracing for their city to be overrun by a militia notorious for committing atrocities.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Dan Kitwood/Getty Images

item.title: ‘Platner Is Vile.’ McConnell Is Missing. Welcome to American Politics.
item.description: What America’s embarrassments have in common.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle, David French, David Wallace-Wells and Derek Arthur
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: illustration by The New York Times, photo by Kent Nishimura/Getty

item.title: We Returned From China. We Realized Our Century’s Biggest Challenge.
item.description: A.I. demands a populist approach that treats the technology as a public project.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmidt and Selina Xu

item.title: We’re Living in a Tick Nightmare. It’s Time to Go to War.
item.description: With ticks, we’ve barely begun to fight back. The longer we wait, the more territory they will claim across both our geographic and psychic maps.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mingle
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: The Population Bust Is Coming Sooner Than Anyone Is Prepared For
item.description: America is not prepared for population decline.
item.dc:creator: Lyman Stone

item.title: Looksmaxxers Actually Don’t Care What Women Think
item.description: Social pressure for men to look a certain way is coming primarily from other men.
item.dc:creator: Kate Manne

item.title: More Than One Way to Fix Social Security
item.description: Readers respond to two guest essays about how to cover the shortfall in the program’s funding before it’s too late.

item.title: ‘Platner Is Vile.’ McConnell Is Missing. Welcome to American Politics.
item.description: What America’s embarrassments have in common.

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Iran Fires on Ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s attack on a container ship came hours after a meeting between the foreign ministers of Iran and Oman about opening the strait to traffic.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz seen from Musandam, Oman, this month. While the Trump administration has said that a cease-fire would fully lift Iran’s blockade of the strait, that has yet to be borne out.
